title: Vision-Language Models Struggle to Align Entities across Modalities
abstract: This paper analyzes the capabilities of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for cross-modal entity linking. The authors introduce a new multimodal question-answering benchmark called MATE, which contains synthetic images of 3D scenes aligned with textual representations. The study finds that while humans achieve almost perfect performance, current VLMs struggle to consistently align attributes across modalities, especially in image-to-text tasks.

methodology.data: Synthetic images of 3D scenes aligned with textual representations
variants: Image-to-text: Identify object based on visual attribute and retrieve name from text
Text-to-image: Locate object using name and determine color from image

results: VLMs and humans show very different behaviors for cross-modal entity linking
VLMs perform worse than humans, especially for image-to-text tasks
VLM performance is influenced by number of objects and target attributes, while human performance is stable
The real challenge is in the cross-modal setting, not within a single modality
